California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
February 5, 1992
Richard R. RudnanskY
city Attorney
city of Petaluna
11 English street
P.o. Box 51
Petaluna, CA 949 53
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-03 6
Dear Mr. Rudnansky:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of Petaluma Mayor M. Patricia HiIIigoss, city
councilriembers Brj.an sobel, Jack w. cavanagh and Michael Davis,
and city Manager John Scharer, regarding their responsibilities
under tire coni I i"t-of- interest provisions of the Political 
Reform
Act (the "Act" ) .1
PleasenotethatnothinginthisletterShouldbeconstrued
to evaluate any conduct that has already taken place' In
addition, any bommission advice is necessarily dependent on the
i""rr.""y'and completeness of the facts presented to us' 
The
comrnission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice.
(In re Ooelsbv (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71')
OUESTIONS
1. May l'Iayor Hilligoss and City Manager Scharer-participate
in aecj.sioni wtrictr rnay niterially 
financially affect -Envirotech
op.i"ti"g 
systens, rni., a whoIly owned- subsidiary of wheelabrator
r'""ir."i"gi.i, 
r.,c., where both Mlyor Hilligoss and city Manager
Scharer formerLy owned stock in Wlreelabrator's parent corporation'
waste Managernent, Inc. ?

2. 
May Councilmembers Sobel, cavanagh and Davis particj'pate
in decisions which nay naterially financially affect Envirotech
Operating Systens, Inc., a wholly owned _ subsidiary of l{heelabrator
f-echnologies, Inc', where aLL the councilmenbers have received
contributions from wheelabrator's Parent corporation, waste
tltanagement, Inc . ?
CONCLUS TONS
1. Dividends paid to t'fayor HiIligoss and city Manager
Scharer on a security vhich is registered with the Securities and
Exchange Commission are not incorne. Proceeds from the sale of
=""u.i€i"s registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission
are not incorni so Iong as (1) t'layor Hilligoss and City uanager
Scharer sotd the securities on a stock exchange, and (2) l{ayor
Hllligoss and City }lanager scharer do not know or have reason to
know ihe identity of the purchaser.
Absent sone other econonic interest in waste l'lanagement, Inc'
or its subsidiaries, litayor Hilligoss and city lilanager scharer nay
participate in decisions which may materialty financially affect
i.laste ulnagenent, rnc. or its subsidiaries?
2. Absent an economic interest in waste Management, Inc' or
its subsidiaries, councilmenbers sobel, cavanagh and Davis may
participate in decisions of the Petaluma city counci.l t hich may
inii".i"i:,y 
financially affect waste Management, Inc' despi'te
havinq releived contributions from waste Management, Inc'
a;*;;ig" 
contributions are expressly excluded from the definition
of i'in;one,' and therefore do not constitute an economic interest
as set forth in the Act.
FACTS
For the Past 13 years Envirotech OPerating Systens, Inc'
(ttEnvirotechul has operated the city sewer plant pursuant to a
iervice contract. Iir tsaa, the citi determined that a new pl-ant
"""fa U" needed and negotiations were entered into with Envirotech
to take on the Project.
In 1991 the city approved a ltemorandum of Understanding
(rrmemorandurntt) betwein tire city and Envirotech' Pursuant to the
;";;;;;;;; 
tire city vould nes6tiate with Envj'rotech for 18 months
,iin i"=p"it to the proposal fo -design, bui1d, own and oPerate the
oiant. 
iou stated that the estiruatei for the construction of the
plant projected a S15-S2o :niIlion do1lar cost'
You anticipate that further negotiati'ons will' be necessary
Uetween-ttre cit| and Envirotech concernino revisions of the
nemorandum. you arso-anii"ipai" that the revised nenorandurn will
;;^;;;;;;-to-ir,e 
-Ltv 
"orn.il ror approval' Finallv' ir all the
;;";;;;i 
iequirernenls are net concerning the rnemorandum' you
:;;r;i;;t; 
tnat tne citv will need to nesotiate a service
"gi...E"t wittr unviroiectr ana conduct heirings on environmental


Our Fi■e NO. A-92-036
Page 3
and land use issues with resPect to the project. 
AII these issues
will ultinately be considered by the city council'
You have becone concerned that some of the officials 
in
ouestion mav have a conflict of interest with respect to the
f,roceedingsl envirotech is wholly owned by another corporation,
Wheelabrator Technologies, Inc. (trl{heelabratorrr ) . waste
Management, Inc. ("Waste Managenentn) is the najority orr'ner of
Wheelabrator. Mayor Hilligoss or.rned 1OO shares of stock in Waste
uanagenent with a value of approxinately S3,700. city Manager
Schaier owned 130 shares of Waste Uanagement stock with a value of
approxiroately $4,800. Each has received stock dividends fron the
sttck ($aa ana $sz per year, respectively). 
In-April.1991, both
ttayor iiltigoss 
and city r'ranager Scharer sold all of their Waste
Managenent 6tock on the open market.
In addition, councilnenbers sobel, cavanagh and Davis have
aII recelved coniributions fron Wheel.abrator's parent corPoration,
l{aste Management.
ANALYSI S
Section 87100 of the Act Provides:
No public official 
at any level of state or
Iocal government shall rnake, participate in making
or in iny way attenpt to use his official 
position
to influlnce a governmental decision in whj-ch he
knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest.
Each of the various officiaLs for which you request advice
are I'oublic officials" 
as defined in the Act. 
(Section 82048')
Thus,'each is prohibited frorn using their official 
position to
influence a governmental decision in which they know or have
reason to know they have a financial interest.
section 871o3 specifies that a public official 
has a
financiat interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a materj'aI financial effect,
distinguishable fron the effect on the public generally, on the
otriciit 
or a member of his or her inunediate faroily or on:
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect investrnent $rorth
one thousand dollars ($1,o00) or more-
***
(c) Any source of incone, other than gifts
and other than loans by a connercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public uithout regard to
official 
status, aggregating tuo hundred fifty


Our Fi■e No. A-92-036
Page 4
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or pronised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
Section 87103 (a) and (c).
Both t[ayor Hilligoss and city uanager Scharer formerly owned
stock in Waste Managernent valued at nore then S1,o00' Ho$/ever,
with the sale of their stock in April, 1991, they no J-onger have
an investnent interest in i{aste trtanagenent pursuant to section
87103(a). You stated that l'layor Hilligoss and city Manager
scharer' received dividends on the stock. In addition, upon the
sale of the stock, the tuo officials 
received proceeds. Moreover,
CounciiuenUers Soiel, Cavanagh and Davis have received carnpaign
contributlons from a corPoration affiliated with Envirotech '
If these paynents are incone, the officials 
would have an
".onoii. 
-it [..1"t in the source oi the pa]rments ' However' section
82030 (b) Provides :
(b) 'rlncone'r also does not include:
(1) Campaign contributions required to
be reported under chapter 4 (commencing with
Section 84 f00) .
tit
(5) Dividends, interest, or any other
return on a security which is registered v,ith
the Securities and Exchange Cornmission of the
United States governnent or a conmodity future
registered with the Cornmodity Futures Trading
Cornmission of the United states government,
except proceeds fron the saLe of these
securities and couunodities futures.
***
(12) Proceeds froD the sale of
securities registered with the Securities and
Exchange Comnission of the United States
government or frorn the sale of commodities
futures registered rrith the Commodj,ty Futures
Trading Conmission of the Unj.ted States
government if the fj.Ler sel1s the securities
or the commodities futures on a stock or
commodities exchange and does not know or have
reason to know the identity of the purchaser.


Thus, pursuant to section 8203o(b) (1), campaign contributions
are expressly exenpted from the definition of 'rincone.r'z (Evmil
Advice Lettei, No. A-86-025.) In addition, if the stock owned by
the mayor and city manager was registered l,,ith the securities and
exchanle Comrnission, Section 82030(b) provides that the definition
of rrinione" does not incLude: (1) dividends, interest, or any
other return on a security, oI., (2) the proceeds froD the sale of
securities on a stock or cornmodities exchange if the official 
sold
it and does not know or have reason to knov the identity of the
purchaser. 3
You have not discussed any other economic Iink between the
officials 
and the corporations in question. consequently, absent
some other economic links to the corporations, the nayor, the
councilnenbers and the city nanager nray participate in decisions
concerning l{aste }lanagenent and its subsidiaries.
If you have any further questions regqli1g this rnatter,
please fiel free to contact toe at (916) 322-5901'r
Our File No. A-92-036
Page 5
S incerely,
Scott
HaIIabrin
General counse I
ace
Legal Division
SH: JwW: aa
Enc I osures
2 Section 84308 does require that officers of appointed boards
abstain frorn decisions concerning licenses, perrnits and
entitlements for use vhere a party or a participant has provided
over S25o in campaign contributions to the official. 
However,
since Section 84308 applies only to appointed officers of state
and local governnent agencies, it would not aPpLy to Petaluna City
Council decisions. Hosever, if the councilmembers are acting as
appointed members of another governnental body, the restrictions
oi section 84308 tdould apply. You should contact us for further
advice should this be the case.
1' 
If the mayor or cj.ty rnanager were to know the ident j'ty of the
purchaser, the purchaser will be a source of incone and the rnayor
and city manager could not participate in decisj.ons vrhich would
have a rnateriat financial effect on the purchaser. Regulation
]-8702.6 has been enclosed for your inforrnation.
' 
copies of Commission regulations and opi,nions are available in
many 1aw libraries. 
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Conmission advice letters nay be obtained from the Cornrnission at a
cost of 10Q per page.
